questions.question: What does "write the mcp" mean here — extend what exists, or start fresh?

options.label: Verify existing MCP works
options.description: tools/meetless-mcp/ already routes to intel /v1/ask. Run reingest, then test the existing tools (query, relationship_verdict) against fresh data. Fix anything broken.

options.label: Extend existing MCP
options.description: Existing MCP stays; add new tools/modes for the new pipeline's outputs (e.g., raw chunk retrieval, claim/relation browsing, ingestion status).

options.label: Write a new MCP
options.description: The existing one queries the old surface. Build a separate MCP that talks directly to the new ingestion pipeline's data (Postgres + Weaviate) for low-level inspection.

questions.question: Which workspace + intel instance for the reingest?

options.label: Local intel + dogfood ws
options.description: Local intel at 127.0.0.1:8100, workspace ws_dogfood_v1 (or current dogfood workspace ID). I'll start intel if it's not running.

options.label: Staging intel
options.description: Point at connector-staging / intel-staging. I'll need the workspace ID + base URL.

options.label: Tell me what to use
options.description: I'll specify the workspace ID and intel URL in my reply.

answers.Which workspace + intel instance for the: local envs. Make sure you can reuse the current dogfood workspace first, to ensure it deoesn't interfere with the evaluation.
